customer_name: Pecks Farm Market II East LLC
site_name: Pecks Farm Market II East LLC

citations.narrative:
Address for 4 Barbado ewes that were sold was not recorded and available for inspection. Record of disposition must
include the name and address of where animals were sold to and can be important for tracking animal health. Ensure to
obtain and keep animal records of disposition as required.
***Correct by 3/20/2026.

Areas that animals can touch had sharp/rough edges that are in need of repair:
-The edge of a shelter in the Barbado area had a rusted and rough edge sticking up.
-The fencing of the bison is curled up and loose on some areas.
-Areas in the fence where the pig lives had a panel with points she can touch, a loose panel on the ground, and some
areas of loose wire strands.
Rough edges, sharp points and loose wires can injure animals. Ensure that all housing facilities are maintained in a
manner that can protect animals from injury.

There is an old rusted barrel with sharp edges in the peacock enclosure. Rusted sharp edges could injure the peacocks.
Ensure that housing areas for birds are safe and free from sharp edges and points.

The cover for the chicken coop (which chickens can shelter in at night per representative) is not intact. A shelter that is not
secure for chickens does not protect them overnight from other animals such as predators getting access to them. Ensure
to maintain an area of safe overnight shelter for the chickens that can protect them from other animals getting access.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

include the name and address of where animals were sold to and can be important for tracking animal health. Ensure to 
obtain and keep animal records of disposition as required.

3.125(a)  Repeat
   
Facilities, general.

-The edge of a shelter in the Barbado area had a rusted and rough edge sticking up.  
-The fencing of the bison is curled up and loose on some areas.  
-Areas in the fence where the pig lives had a panel with points she can touch, a loose panel on the ground, and some 
areas of loose wire strands.  
Rough edges, sharp points and loose wires can injure animals. Ensure that all housing facilities are maintained in a 
manner that can protect animals from injury. 
3.153(a)(1)(i)  Repeat
   
Primary enclosures. 
There is an old rusted barrel with sharp edges in the peacock enclosure. Rusted sharp edges could injure the peacocks. 
Ensure that housing areas for birds are safe and free from sharp edges and points. 
3.153(a)(1)(iv)  Repeat
   
Primary enclosures. 
The cover for the chicken coop (which chickens can shelter in at night per representative) is not intact. A shelter that is not 
secure for chickens does not protect them overnight from other animals such as predators getting access to them. Ensure 
to maintain an area of safe overnight shelter for the chickens that can protect them from other animals getting access.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
